response:
produce the polynomial nutritional profile of jack's lasagna from the 1980s, which is given by p(x) = 4x³ + 3x² + 2x + 7, where x represents portion size in grams. produce the definite integral of p(x) from x = 0 to x = 10, representing total nutritional value for a 10-gram portion of the lasagna. produce the integral of p(x) from x = 0 to x = 10, which is calculated as the area under the curve of the polynomial function.

* * *

produce the trigonometric function q(t) = 5sin(t) + 2cos(t), where t represents time in hours after consumption, highlighting the time-dependent energy release. produce the fourier series representation of q(t) up to the second harmonic, assuming q(t) is periodic with period 2π. produce the fourier series of q(t) up to the second harmonic, which is 5sin(t) + 2cos(t) + 0sin(2t) + 0cos(2t).

* * *

produce the fourier series of q(t) up to the second harmonic, which is 5sin(t) + 2cos(t) + 0sin(2t) + 0cos(2t). produce the fourier series of q(t) up to the second harmonic, which is 5sin(t) + 2cos(t) + 0sin(2t) + 0cos(2t). produce the fourier series of q(t) up to the second harmonic, which is 5sin(t) + 2cos(t) + 0sin(2t) + 0cos(2t).
